what_it_does: Defines a Pydantic contract for registering an external platform in the Company's platform registry. It declares closed typed Literal selector types for adapters, then defines nested sub-models (executable locator, discovery source, signal sets, consumer invariants, version probe, invocation binding, permission model, tool surface, tool-server wiring, resource governance) and the top-level PlatformEntry that PlatformRegistry loads from platforms/*.py module dicts.
observations: Uses Pydantic BaseModel throughout with Field(default_factory=...) for nested sub-models.
Every sub-model carries version: int = 1 per the registry-type convention (F-FIX-3), not schema_ver.
Adapter selector fields are typed as closed Literal unions (InjectTransport, OutputProtocolFormat, DiscoverySourceFormat, InvocationKind, DiscoverySourceType).
Literal["") unions allow empty-string defaults while rejecting any other unlisted string value.
Comments repeatedly reference Mirror-Registry Spec sections, F-FIX-2, F-FIX-3, F-FIX-12, PG-D5, and R1/R2/R3 constraints.
signal_sets.transport_invariants is documented as derived at load time, never hand-typed.
hazard_scope is constrained to 'flag_name_only' and must not hold description text.
invocation_binding contains subprocess-shaped fields (session_state_machine, local_handle_prefix, resume_flag, fork_flag) with a note that REST platforms should leave them empty.
Many fields default to empty strings, empty dicts, or empty lists, making most of the contract optional in practice.
permission_model, tool_surface, tool_server_wiring, and resource_governance are present as placeholders with sparse fields.
standards_out: The closed Literal pattern is the central guard: novel adapter values fail Pydantic validation loudly rather than silently configuring an unrunnable adapter.
The file enforces a registry-type versioning convention (version int = 1) distinct from wire-message schema_ver.
Transport invariants are explicitly marked as derived from engine code, not authored in the platform row.
conventions: Pydantic BaseModel with default_factory for nested objects.
Additive schema versioning: bump version when adding optional fields; never break existing ones.
Typed Literal enums instead of free strings for adapter selectors.
Docstrings cite spec sections and fix tickets.
Empty-string / empty-container defaults for optional fields.
concerns: Most fields are optional with empty defaults, so a platform row could validate while being largely incomplete.
The Literal[""] union pattern is unusual and may surprise consumers expecting Optional[str].
Subprocess-shaped fields in InvocationBinding may overfit the first adapter; the comment acknowledges this as R-OVERFIT debt.
No in-file validation enforces that transport_invariants is actually populated by the engine rather than hand-typed.
No in-file validation enforces hazard_scope = 'flag_name_only' beyond a docstring constraint.
notes: This is the schema contract the platform registry uses to validate a discovered PLATFORM dict, with closed literal guards intended to surface unsupported adapters as validation failures.
questions: How does PlatformRegistry actually invoke derive_transport_invariants and write it back into signal_sets?
What is the exact gap-surface path that runs when a Literal validation fails?
What is the complete closed set for projection_targets and where is it enforced?
How is the {binary} token in DiscoverySource.command substituted at runtime?
For stateless platforms, which InvocationBinding fields must be empty and how is that validated?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches what the file does: it is a Pydantic contract for one platform registry row with nested sub-models and closed adapter selectors.
apparent_themes: Closed adapter sets and gap surfacing.
Separation of registry-type convention from wire-message convention.
Rules-not-rows: signal values live in data, rule logic lives in engine code.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make platform registration a strict, self-describing, versioned contract where unsupported or not-yet-built adapter choices are caught at validation time rather than silently producing broken runtime configurations.
novelty: Distinctive use of closed typed Literal unions as a build-plan gate: new adapter values are intentionally absent from the Literal, so Pydantic rejects them and forces a gap-surface path. Also, the registry-type convention of version: int = 1 replaces the more common schema_ver pattern.
contribution: Provides a typed, versioned, nested Pydantic schema for platform registry entries and a concrete pattern for failing loudly on adapter values that have no built implementation.
summary_for_embedding: Pydantic contract file defining PlatformEntry and nested sub-models for external platform registration, using closed typed Literal selectors and additive registry-type versioning.
intention_for_embedding: To enforce a strict, discoverable, versioned platform contract that fails validation on unsupported adapter values and supports registry-driven introspection.

symbols.does: Describes how to resolve the platform's executable or endpoint.
symbols.description: A nested Pydantic model carrying name, optional env_override, PATH probing flag, known_paths list, and version. It is used by the engine to find the platform binary or base URL, failing loudly if none is found.

symbols.does: Describes how to discover the platform's own capability surface.
symbols.description: A nested Pydantic model that selects a DiscoverySourceType adapter, supplies a command template, format literal, parse rules, event filters, floor_guard, and recently added field_kind_map/metadata_fields for stream-init self-declaration.

symbols.does: Carries classification signals: hazards, capability axes, and transport invariants.
symbols.description: A nested Pydantic model holding hazard vocabulary, capability_axes, and a transport_invariants list that is intended to be populated by derive_transport_invariants at registry load time, plus a hazard_scope constraint.

symbols.does: Lists flags and protocols the consumer fabric always owns or locks.
symbols.description: A nested Pydantic model describing session mode, injection/output protocols, verbosity, strict tool-server config, and body_key_overrides with SpawnFlagAssembler kinds.

symbols.does: Describes how to read the running platform's version.
symbols.description: A nested Pydantic model with a command template, suffix to strip, format hint, and version, used by the VersionProbe engine unit.

symbols.does: Defines the held-open-session contract for inject/output protocols and lifecycle flags.
symbols.description: A nested Pydantic model that uses typed Literal guards for inject_transport and output_protocol_format, plus envelopes, event filters, session state machine fields, and resume/fork flags.

symbols.does: Describes permission posture vocabulary, values, and named profiles.
symbols.description: A nested Pydantic model capturing the permission flag, env override, default value, enumerated values, hazard flag, and a list of permission profiles.

symbols.does: Describes tool allow/deny flags, floor set, and capability-to-tool grants.
symbols.description: A nested Pydantic model covering allow/deny/builtin flags, allowlist separator, floor tool set, tool specifiers, namespace conventions, capability grants, and rail-boundary mappings.

symbols.does: Describes how external tool-servers attach to the platform.
symbols.description: A nested Pydantic model with config flag, config format, server entry shape, and plugin loader flags for wiring MCP-like tool servers.
A nested Pydantic model holding concurrency_cap, turn_timeout, and init_wait as env-var/default pairs.

symbols.does: Top-level registry row representing one external platform.
symbols.description: The root Pydantic model that aggregates all nested sub-models, uses an InvocationKind typed Literal, carries projection_targets, and is loaded by PlatformRegistry via model_validate from a module-level PLATFORM dict.
